name: prospect-identification
description: Given a company description, identify the two most precise business types to prospect into and two decision maker titles most likely to book meetings

businessTypes.description: Two types or stages of businesses separated by 'and', max 7 words, lowercase

decisionMakers.description: Two plural decision maker job titles separated by 'and', max 6 words, lowercase, must be plural

business_type_accuracy.description: The two business types accurately represent who this company sells to. Specific and precise, not generic. Matches actual ICP for this company.

decision_maker_accuracy.description: The two DM titles are the actual buyers for this company's product. Recognizable titles, not obscure. Right level of seniority.

label_normalization.description: Language is casual and natural. No formal titles spelled out (CMOs not Chief Marketing Officers). Lowercase unless acronym. Like talking to a friend.

format_compliance.description: businessTypes ≤7 words. decisionMakers ≤6 words. Both use plural titles. Separated by 'and'. No capitals except acronyms.

rubric.scoring: 1-5 per dimension, weighted average normalized to 0-1. 5 = indistinguishable from expert prospector output. 1 = wrong or missing.
